config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, or unfair treatment. In my view, a healthy society is built by protecting basic individual rights first, then balancing them with the common good when there is a real conflict. From my perspective, the state and the majority should not be trusted to decide people’s rights too freely.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kepentingan bersama,” hasilnya sering berupa penyensoran, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan wewenang. Hak individu memang tidak absolut dalam setiap situasi, tetapi menurut saya hak dasar harus menjadi batas yang jelas, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan demi tujuan kolektif. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menekan individu demi ketertiban, melainkan yang melindungi kebebasan pribadi sambil membatasi konflik dengan aturan yang adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because in practice that often becomes an excuse for the majority or the state to silence people. From an Indonesian perspective, I am especially cautious about this, because appeals to “public order” or “common good” can be used to pressure minorities, limit speech, or excuse unfair treatment. Society does matter, but it should be protected through fair rules that respect basic rights first, not by treating the individual as disposable. If there is a real conflict, the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan daripada hak individu secara umum. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman sejarah di sini menunjukkan bahwa saat “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai terlalu longgar, itu sering berubah jadi pembenaran untuk sensor, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam keadaan tertentu, tetapi batasnya harus sangat jelas dan sempit, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan kebebasan pribadi demi kenyamanan mayoritas. Masyarakat yang sehat justru bergantung pada perlindungan hak dasar, karena tanpa itu “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah sekali berarti kepentingan orang yang paling kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia that idea can easily be turned into pressure from the majority or the state. I have seen how phrases like public order and common good can be used to silence minorities or excuse unfair treatment, so I do not trust society to define its own interests without strong limits. Society is important, but it is healthier when individual rights are the baseline and any restriction has to be narrowly justified. If we reverse that order, the people with the least power are usually the first to lose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengambil prioritas atas hak individu secara umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat pola yang sama berulang: begitu “demi kebaikan bersama” diberi kuasa terlalu besar, itu sering berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk sensor, diskriminasi, dan penyalahgunaan wewenang oleh negara atau mayoritas. Hak individu memang tidak selalu absolut, tetapi justru harus menjadi batas yang kuat, bukan sesuatu yang mudah dikorbankan demi kenyamanan kolektif. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan hak dasar terlebih dahulu, lalu pembatasan yang benar-benar sempit dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan ketika ada konflik nyata.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
